description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a dark, almost black coloration and a matte, rough surface. The stone has a somewhat wedge-like profile, tapering to a thinner edge on one side and broadening out on the opposite end. Its surface is uneven, with subtle undulations, minor chips, and natural textural variations typical of weathered rock. There are no visible artificial carvings or markings except for a small area of white alphanumeric text on one side, possibly for cataloging or identification. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park, garden, or roadside setting.
description: A dark, irregularly shaped stone with a rough, matte surface and a wedge-like profile, featuring minor chips and a small area of white text.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and blocky, with a rough, uneven surface and visible white alphanumeric text on the right side.
description_view_1: Seen edge-on, the stone looks thin and elongated, highlighting its wedge-like taper and narrow profile.
description_view_2: This side shows the stone's broadest face, with a consistent dark coloration and subtle surface undulations, lacking visible markings.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the stone's thinness and irregular outline.
attribute_location: urban park, roadside verge, or landscaped garden
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: No distinct pattern; surface is irregular with natural mottling and minor chips, and a small area of white text.
attribute_weathering: Rough and matte, with minor chips and surface undulations indicating moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural ground cover or landscaping materials, but the dark color and catalog text may contrast with manicured urban environments.
